title: International Day of Yoga: Visakhapatnam gears up with Yogandhra sessions ahead of June 21 RK Beach event
content: A Yogandhra session at the Naval Dockyard opens the run-up to the International Day of Yoga celebrations at RK Beach on June 21

title: ‘It is only through Bharathiraja’s films that we can see what the villages of Tamil Nadu were once like’
content: “It was Bharathiraja who, for the first time, brought the villages of southern Tamil Nadu — especially those around Madurai — alive before our eyes”

title: En iniya Thamizh makkale: unforgettable celluloid legacy of a son of the soil
content: Bharathiraja  inspired his assistant directors to carve their own path, and K. Bhagyaraj, R. Parthiban and Pandiarajan all evolved from the Bharathiraja school of thought

title: Trinamool Congress MP Prakash Chik Barik resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: I will work as per CM Suvendu Adhikari’s instructions in the future; I will work for the development, says Prakash Chik Barik

title: Mortar-like object found near river in Kanpur Dehat; bomb disposal team from Jhansi rushes in
content: The Superindentent of Police said the bomb detection team has prima facie declared it as non-explosive

title: CPI not part of DMK alliance in Tamil Nadu, says Veerapandian
content: “The current situation is not conducive for an alliance. In the coming days, when there are local body and other elections, the CPI will decide its stand. The CPI takes its own political decisions,” he said

title: India's economic growth rate to weaken at 6.6% in FY27 on slower investments, consumption: BMI
content: Business Monitor International's projection is in line with Reserve Bank of India's 6.6% growth estimates for FY27

title: Kerala on Nipah alert after Kozhikode man’s preliminary test turns positive
content: Lab results of his body fluid samples sent to National Institute of Virology in Pune for Nipah infection are awaited

title: Monsoon 2026 tracker LIVE: After strong onset, southwest monsoon to enter a sluggish phase in Kerala
content: The Meteorological Centre issued an orange alert for hailstorm, lightning and gusty winds in parts of Himachal Pradesh on June 11 and 12

title: Bharathiraja passes away LIVE | People pay homage to Bharathiraja’s mortal remains
content: A towering personality in the film industry, Bharathiraja was fondly known as Iyakkunar Imayam (Pinnacle among Directors)

title: India’s E85 fuel rollout: what it means for motorists
content: The Central Government’s E85 fuel push ramps up with 500 outlets planned by December 2026, but vehicle compatibility remains a challenge. The move may reduce imports, but not without costs for vehicle owners

title: Sharmila accuses ECI of acting in BJP’s interest over Meenakshi Natarajan nomination
content: The APCC president says the rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination shows constitutional bodies being bent to political ends

title: In Bengaluru, a garbage crisis deepens
content: The is a four-part series on Bengaluru’s mounting solid waste management woes, compounded by an ineffective garbage collection system.

title: Andhra Pradesh SSC exam data correction deadline extended to June 15
content: Headmasters must verify and correct students’ particulars through HM Logins by June 15, after which no requests will be accepted, the Director of Government Examinations says

title: Bond over Board: How board game nights are building a community in Visakhapatnam
content: A growing board game community in Visakhapatnam is creating spaces for friendship, conversation and screen-free fun

title: Heavy rain lashes central Andhra Pradesh; Vijayawada North records 102 mm
content: The overnight rain breaks the heat across NTR, Krishna and Guntur districts, and the disaster management authority forecasts more for Rayalaseema on Thursday

title: 265 lost or stolen mobile phones traced, returned to owners in Kalaburagi
content: Complaints of theft or loss of mobile phones are registered in the Central Equipment Identity Register (CEIR) portal

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 fever grips Thiruvananthapuram as Messi and Ronaldo fans prepare for football’s biggest stage
content: Public screenings, prediction contests and towering flex boards signal the return of football’s most celebrated tournament

title: ‘The tragedy isn’t just what happens to the women, but also the emotional death of the men who feel compelled to inflict it’
content: Fathi Salim, the author of Dechoma and the Women of Mahé (Bloomsbury Publishing), on her debut novel, which has recently been translated into English by J Devika

title: Alert issued against Nipah in Kerala from April to September
content: Previous outbreaks and spillover cases suggest that the virus is found to have been active in the State in the the six-month period starting in April; health officials lay focus on Kozhikode, Malappuram, Palakkad, and Thrissur districts

title: Supreme Court to hear Meenakshi Natarajan’s plea against rejection of RS candidature on June 12
content: Senior advocate Abhishek Singhvi urged the Bench to consider the unlisted mentioning of her plea on the ground that the nomination paper was wrongly rejected by the returning officer

title: EAC-PM recommends targeted splitting of seats for delimitation, shows model allowing 50% rise for all large States
content: The advisory council says it assembled a dataset on elections to LS seats from 2009 to 2024 to estimate a ‘statistical relationship’ between voter turnout, constituency size, and five compositional features of the constituency; the model results in increasing the size of the Lok Sabha to 824 seats and it is broadly in line with what the Centre had suggested in April, when it brought the Delimitation-related Bills but failed to pass them in Parliament

title: Global study sought lessons, not a single model for voter registration: Karnataka CEO
content: The study examined voter registration systems followed by 87 Election Management Bodies (EMBs) across North America, Latin America and the Caribbean, Europe, Africa, Asia and Oceania

title: Nationwide stir over exam irregularities: Cockroach Janta Party to release education manifesto
content: Mr. Dipke said with the launch of a nationwide campaign from Pune, they will take the movement to various cities, including Jaipur, Lucknow, Amritsar and Bengaluru

title: Manipur government will not remain mute spectator to atrocities: CM Khemchand on ‘killing’ of Naga hostages
content: The United Naga Council has imposed a 24-hour shutdown in parts of Manipur from 6 a.m. on June 11 to protest against the killing of Naga hostages

title: More than 30 kg heroin recovered in Punjab; six held
content: An FIR is registered under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act at Chheharta police station in Amritsar

title: BJP demands probe into new garbage tenders in Bengaluru; Karnataka CM calls LoP ‘spokesperson for garbage mafia’
content: Leader of Opposition R. Ashok lodged a complaint with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot and Karnataka Lokayukta alleging a ‘₹39,000 crore scam with a ₹10,000 crore kickback’

title: Naidu, Pawan Kalyan, Lokesh hail Modi’s record 4,399 consecutive days in office
content: A striking feature of Modi’s journey has been the balance between welfare and development, says the A.P. CM

title: Rebuilding a century-old library in Kerala book by book
content: The Sree Chitra Thirunal Grandhasala is a rich repository of nearly 2 lakh rare books, old manuals, magazines, and other out-of-print publications that have been lost to history.

title: Ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi’s daughter T. Veena seeks postponement of ED questioning in CMRL pay-off case
content: T. Veena cites health grounds. She has reportedly mailed ED expressing her willingness to appear later and cooperate with the investigation by submitting relevant documents through her lawyer

title: Bharathiraja, the master filmmaker: How the ‘Iyakkunar Imayam’ captured the soul of Tamil Nadu for posterity
content: From ‘16 Vayathinile’ to ‘Vedham Pudhithu,’ ‘Iyakkunar Imayam’ Bharathiraja, who passed away today, made some of the most important Tamil films of all time

title: Arguing before a trial court is like playing a Test match, while appeals are like IPL matches: Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan dissent with a view taken by Justice Vikram Nath of Supreme Court during his stint as a judge of Allahabad High Court

title: Journalist bodies criticise freezing of NewsClick accounts by I-T Department
content: ‘The salaries of all employees, including support staff, cannot be disbursed, including for the 19 days of work in December,’ a joint statement said

title: FEMA adjudicating authority imposes ₹184 crore penalty on NewsClick, founder Prabir Purkayastha
content: The adjudication proceedings were initiated on a complaint under Section 16 of FEMA filed by the ED alleging contraventions of FEMA and the rules and regulations pertaining to the receipt and utilisation of foreign funds

title: Second-life surgery | Surrendered Maoists in Maharashtra reverse vasectomies for family life
content: As men in Maharashtra’s Maoist-affected Gadchiroli district give up arms, their outlook on life has changed. This report discusses how they are reversing the vasectomies that a life of resistance in the forest had forced them into

title: Supreme Court finds NewsClick founder Prabir Purkayastha’s arrest invalid, says he is entitled to be released from custody
content: Communication of grounds of arrest or detention in writing by the investigating agency or police was ‘sacrosanct and cannot be breached under any situation,’ the apex court held

title: Director Bharathiraja (1941-2026)
content: Renowned Tamil film director Bharathiraja passed away in Chennai on Wednesday (June 10, 2026). He was 84.

title: Strong heroines, mellow heroes, engaging stories and fierce social commentary define Bharathiraja’s repertoire
content: With fresh eyes, groomed on a rural story line in the lush surroundings of Theni in south Tamil Nadu, Bharathiraja decided he was done with the conventional tropes of the hero, the baddie, and a hanger-on heroine

title: Dreaded don to dear grandfather: Actor Bharathiraja’s many faces
content: From ‘Kurangu Bommai’ and ‘Rocky’ to ‘Thiruchitrambalam’ and ‘Karumegangal Kalaigindrana,’ here are five of the best performances of the ‘actor’ Bharathiraja from recent years that reintroduced him to modern audiences

title: Firmly opposed to attacks on merchant shipping: India tells UNSC amid ongoing Iran conflict
content: Parvathaneni said India is firmly opposed to attacks on merchant shipping, as many of its nationals are prominent in its global workforce

title: India summons U.S. Deputy Chief of Mission after second ship attack in three days
content: “We condemn the attack on the commercial vessel Settebello off the coast of Oman,” the Ministry of External Affairs said in a statement.

title: Two Indian seafarers dead, one missing after U.S. attack on ship off Oman, says sailors’ union
content: India condemns attack, summons U.S. Embassy’s Deputy Chief of Mission Jason Meeks to lodge a “strong protest”

title: Kerala rains: IMD issues orange alert in 2 districts; predicts thunderstorms, strong winds across State
content: On Tuesday, heavy rains accompanied by strong winds lashed several parts of Kerala, particularly in northern districts, leading to widespread waterlogging and prompting precautionary evacuation measures in some areas

title: What is lost and gained in NFHS-6
content: NFHS-6 records gains in child nutrition, maternal care, institutional births and women’s internet use, while also introducing new questions on digital literacy and direct benefit transfers; but the survey’s preliminary fact sheet is thinner than the last round, dropping key indicators such as anaemia, mortality, sex ratio at birth

title: 3 injured in two suspected elephant attacks in Kerala’s Palakkad
content: The suspected elephant attacks occurred a day after a 65-year-old man was killed in a wild elephant attack in Wayanad district

title: Snake Speek Project from Andhra Pradesh among top 50 at National Geographic Slingshot Challenge
content: After global recognition at the National Geographic Society’s 2026 Slingshot Challenge, Snake Speek plans to expand its snake conservation and awareness efforts

title: ‘Guess the climax if you can’: The day Bharathiraja challenged film critics
content: In a 2008’s press screening of Bommalattam in 2008, Bharathiraja threw a challenge to journalists: predict the climax if you can

title: Three Indian sailors killed after US strike on oil tanker off Oman near Strait of Hormuz
content: Two Indian sailors have died and a chief engineer is missing after a commercial vessel, MT Settebello, was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz. While 21 of the 24 Indian crew members were rescued, the incident raises serious concerns for seafarers in the volatile Gulf region. India has formally protested the attack, emphasizing the need to protect maritime workers.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi told to stay away from professional managers
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth has lauded teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who recently became the youngest player selected for an Indian men's team. Sreesanth emphasized Suryavanshi's commitment and perseverance, urging the 15-year-old to focus solely on cricket and avoid distractions from professional managers, trusting his family to handle off-field matters.

title: Day after Kuki hostages’ release, 6 abducted Nagas found dead in Manipur
content: Six Naga villagers, abducted four weeks ago by Kuki groups, were found dead in Kangpokpi district, Manipur. This discovery has reignited ethnic tensions, prompting a 24-hour shutdown call by the United Naga Council. The UNC demands justice, including abrogating pacts with Kuki militants and declaring KNF-P a terrorist outfit.

title: 'Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you': Palantir CEO message to Altman
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: Delhi weather: 41°C on paper, but it feels like 48°C
content: Delhi sweltered under a scorching 48.2 degrees Celsius 'feels-like' temperature on Wednesday. While an orange alert for rain and thunderstorms has been issued for Thursday, relief might be temporary as humidity persists.  Temperatures are expected to drop significantly by Friday, offering a much-needed respite from the intense heat.

title: AWS' marketing head sends 'message' for laid off Meta employees in meeting
content: AWS marketing head Julia White urged employees to recruit recently laid-off Meta workers, citing around 160 open marketing positions. She acknowledged compensation is a factor in departures but not the primary one, with career growth and lifestyle also contributing. AWS is also working to improve collaboration within its marketing division, moving away from a siloed operating model.

title: Maharashtra woman ploughs field in bullock’s place; govt steps in with a bull
content: A farmer's wife, Hausabai, stepped in to plough their land after lightning killed one of their bulls. The poignant images of her plight gained traction on social media, prompting swift action from chief minister Devendra Fadnavis. Officials provided the family with a new bull and financial aid, offering much-needed relief from their debt-ridden struggle.

title: 10 bullets in 5 seconds: CCTV captures chilling murder outside Haryana gym
content: Fitness trainer Kapil Redhu was shot dead outside his gym in Haryana's Hansi by two assailants on a motorcycle. The brazen daylight execution, captured on CCTV, saw the attacker fire nine bullets at close range. The Lawrence Bishnoi gang later claimed responsibility for the murder, alleging Redhu had ignored prior warnings.

title: Watch - Blasts rock Robat Karim near Tehran as US targets alleged IRGC command centre: Report
content: The US conducted fresh strikes targeting Iran's IRGC command-and-control infrastructure, escalating tensions. These "self-defense strikes" hit military surveillance, communication, and air defense sites across Iran, including near Tehran and in Hormozgan province. The attacks follow previous US strikes after Iran allegedly downed a US helicopter.

title: Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
content: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: How the Indian Navy's new GPS jammers could fool enemy missiles, drones and navigation systems
content: India's Ministry of Defence has procured 20 advanced GNSS jammers, featuring 75% indigenous content, for Rs 449 crore. This strategic acquisition bolsters maritime security and electronic warfare, enabling the Indian Navy to counter threats by disrupting adversary satellite navigation systems like GPS. The move addresses rising interference incidents and enhances operational resilience across all domains.

title: How the C-295 transport aircraft will add value to IAF
content: India's aerospace sector achieved a significant milestone with the first flight of the India-assembled C-295 aircraft. This $3.5 billion program with Airbus will see 40 aircraft assembled locally, boosting domestic manufacturing and replacing aging An-32s. Additional orders for the Navy and Coast Guard highlight the C-295's versatility and India's growing defense production capabilities.

title: 'Bengal's mandate was in favour of BJP': TMC leader Prakash Baraik explains why he quit Rajya Sabha
content: Trinamool Congress MP Prakash Chik Baraik resigned from Rajya Sabha, citing the West Bengal mandate favouring BJP. He stated he would follow CM Suvendu Adhikari's lead, denying BJP pressure. This marks the third TMC MP's exit this week, deepening internal rebellion and raising concerns about the party's stability and parliamentary strength.

title: Air bases, naval hubs, radar sites: Military targets hit by the US and Iran in latest exchange
content: In a dramatic escalation of hostilities, military operations between the United States and Iran have flared anew, with U.S. forces targeting pivotal Iranian infrastructure across the Middle East.

title: 'LPG well covered, crude not a problem': How much energy supplies is India left with?
content: Indian refiners have secured sufficient crude oil and LPG supplies to last through August and mid-July respectively, mitigating concerns from the ongoing Middle East crisis. Purchases from ADNOC and other suppliers, including significant crude acquisitions from the UAE and Brazil, have bolstered inventories. India and the UAE are also expanding strategic oil reserves to enhance energy security.

title: 'Grave error in judgement': Bill Gates says he regrets meetings with Jeffrey Epstein
content: Bill Gates admitted to a US House panel that his meetings with Jeffrey Epstein were a "grave error in judgment," expressing deep regret if his association lent the disgraced financier any credibility. Gates stated he should never have met Epstein and that the financier allegedly used sensitive personal information to pressure him to reconnect, though he never witnessed criminal conduct.

title: IND A vs AFG A: Sooryavanshi's 200-strike-rate knock leaves fans wanting more
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dazzled with a blistering 44 off 22 balls, striking nine fours at a strike rate of 200, as India A built a strong platform against Afghanistan A. The 15-year-old shared a 74-run opening stand with Prabhsimran Singh before falling to Abdullah Ahmadzai. Prabhsimran remained unbeaten on 82, guiding India A to 163/2 after 20.4 overs.

title: How a Google Maps detour cost four UP Police aspirants their future
content: What began as a routine journey to a police recruitment examination turned into a costly detour for four aspirants in Uttar Pradesh. After allegedly following incorrect navigation directions, they reached a location far from their designated centre and missed the test. The episode has sparked questions about dependence on digital maps and the need for better exam-centre location systems.

title: ‘Welcome To The Jungle’ trailer released: Akshay Kumar steals the show in the movie
content: The much-awaited trailer of Welcome To The Jungle, the third instalment of the beloved Welcome franchise, is finally out and has received a warm response. Akshay Kumar is an absolute show stealer, with his reunion with Suniel Shetty being a major highlight. The film releases in cinemas on June 26, 2026.

title: Who is Pranit More? Meet the comedian and former 'Bigg Boss 19' contestant
content: Indian comedian Pranit More, known for his relatable humor and 'Bigg Boss 19' stint, is back in the spotlight after a viral clip sparked debate. His observational comedy, drawing from everyday Indian life, has garnered a significant following. More recently apologized after a show segment involving a biryani remark caused online backlash.

title: Quote of the day by Lady Gaga
content: Lady Gaga, celebrated pop sensation and 16-time Grammy recipient, delivered an inspiring thought during her 2015 conversation with Billboard: "Once you start asking ‘Do I really desire this?’ it’s a game changer; it leads to empowerment and self-realization." This message resonates deeply, emphasizing the journey toward self-discovery and independence in a world filled with external expectations.

title: Amid 'Peddi' row, Madhoo says her film 'Phool Aur Kaante' glorified 'eve-teasing'
content: Madhoo, the celebrated veteran actress, opens up about her role in the 1991 blockbuster 'Phool Aur Kaante', recognizing that it unwittingly celebrated eve-teasing—an idea that feels completely outdated in today's world. She comments on the dramatic shift in cinematic storytelling, where graphic depictions of assault have become taboo while society redefines romance versus harassment. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 30 million barrels of oil storage: India, UAE to expand strategic reserves
content: As India moves to strengthen its energy security amid persistent geopolitical risks in the Middle East, New Delhi and Abu Dhabi have started implementing initiatives announced during PM Modi's visit to the UAE last month.

title: Another big blow to Mamata: Rajya Sabha MP Prakash Chik Baraik quits amid TMC turmoil
content: Another blow strikes the TMC as Rajya Sabha MP Prakash Chik Baraik resigns, marking the third departure from the Upper House recently. This follows the exits of Sukhendu Sekhar Roy and Sushmita Dev, the latter also leaving the party and sparking speculation of a move to the BJP after meeting Assam's Chief Minister.

title: Gold, silver price: Will precious metals continue to tank? Check outlook for today
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold prices are seeing a bearish structure with the downtrend in place. Silver prices are trading cautiously, though with a negative bias, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: 10 bullets fired at Haryana gym owner during workout, chilling video surfaces
content: A gym owner in Haryana's Hansi was shot dead by bike-borne assailants, with the Lawrence Bishnoi gang claiming responsibility. A social media post attributed to 'Hari Boxer' stated they executed the killing, alleging the victim was involved in previous firing incidents. Police are investigating the claim and searching for the shooters.

title: TS Inter Supplementary Result 2026 out: Check link and steps to download TGBIE marks
content: Exciting news for Telangana students! The Board of Intermediate Education is expected to release the IPASE 2026 supplementary exam results today. Candidates who took the exams for first and second-year Intermediate courses can now check their marks online. The supplementary exams, held from May 13 to May 21 for both General and Vocational streams, offered a chance to improve scores.

title: The Siraj workload question: How India created cricket's busiest fast bowler
content: Mohammed Siraj's withdrawal from the England and Ireland T20Is raised a curious question: how could a bowler who had drifted out of India's white-ball plans suddenly need rest? The answer lies in the format India have simply refused to play without him. While Jasprit Bumrah's workload was carefully managed, Siraj quietly became India's default fast bowler and the busiest quick in world cricket. For three years, he has been India's workhorse. Now, workload management has finally reached him too.

title: Trump asked if he is concerned about latest inflation numbers. His answer: 'I love it'
content: President Trump expressed an unexpectedly positive view on recent inflation data, "loving" the rise in consumer prices despite it being the fastest annual pace in over three years. He attributed the increase primarily to energy costs linked to the Iran conflict, claiming his administration's actions would stabilize oil markets and ease inflationary pressures.

title: Pranit More clip goes viral: MBBS students’ remarks on male cadavers spark backlash
content: Comedian Pranit More's show is again embroiled in controversy after a clip featuring Sejal Pawar discussing deceased male bodies went viral. Pawar has since apologized, calling it a learning experience. This follows a previous incident involving a remark about a Rs 370 biryani, sparking debates on humor, respect, and professional conduct.

title: Watch: Dhoni flips coin in Jharkhand T20 league after missing IPL 2026
content: MS Dhoni returned to a cricket ground for the first time since missing IPL 2026 due to injury, attending the opening ceremony of the inaugural Jharkhand T20 League in Ranchi. The former India captain flipped the ceremonial coin and received a warm welcome from fans. His presence boosted the six-team tournament, which will feature 27 matches at the JSCA International Stadium Complex over two weeks.

title: Shooting outside Guru Randhawa’s gym: Police says ‘2 unknown bike-born persons’ fired
content: Panic erupted outside singer Guru Randhawa's Delhi gym this morning as two masked individuals on a bike fired several shots at the establishment's glass. Fortunately, no injuries were reported. Police have launched a swift investigation, forming multiple teams to identify and apprehend the culprits, while CCTV footage is being meticulously examined to uncover the motive behind the brazen act.

title: 'Saying goodbye to our colleagues': Opendoor shuts India operations, hundreds lose jobs
content: American digital real estate firm Opendoor is ceasing its India operations, impacting its nearly 250 employees in the country. CEO Kaz Nejatian confirmed the move, stating it's part of a strategic shift to bring roles closer to American customers under the new Opendoor 2.0 model. This decision, driven by technological advancements and streamlining, is not a reflection of employee performance

title: AIADMK setback continues: P Benjamin joins DMK along with his supporters in Chennai
content: Former AIADMK minister P Benjamin has joined the DMK with his supporters, marking a significant political shift. This move adds to the ongoing exodus of leaders from the opposition party in Tamil Nadu. Benjamin, a former deputy mayor and minister, switched sides in the presence of Chief Minister MK Stalin.

title: Biggest Investing Mistakes In 2026: Why Investors Keep Falling For Bad Stocks
content: According to experts, even experienced market participants are falling into traps despite spending years analysing markets.

title: Third Ship With  Indian Sailors On Fire Near Oman
content: The incident was confirmed by the Indian Embassy in Oman, which said it was closely monitoring the situation and coordinating with the local authorities for further details.

title: "I Worry Something Will Go Wrong": Anthropic CEO On AI
content: Anthropic co-founder and CEO Dario Amodei is very vocal about his fears that AI could get out of hand.

title: How Indians Are Training AI Robots To Take On Household Jobs In The Future
content: Artificial intelligence chatbots and image generators crunch reams of digital data, but building systems to navigate real-life environments is more challenging.

title: "Stay Safe": UK Cops Warn Of Unrest In Belfast Over "Hit List" Of Migrants
content: Police warned that those creating or sharing the list could be committing a criminal offence and deployed 200 additional officers to help prevent further violence.

title: Opinion: Opinion: Courage Or Folly? Inside Rahul Gandhi's Big Madhya Pradesh Gamble
content: How the Congress chose confrontation over compromise, even at the risk of going one down in the Rajya Sabha

title: Punjab Doctor Found Dead, Family Blames Husband's Affair, Assault
content: The woman had been living alone since July 2025 as she was fed up of her husband's physical assault, her family said.

title: Video Captures Moment When US Struck Tanker Carrying Indian Crew
content: A missile tore through the engine room of the oil tanker Settebello, setting off a fire onboard. The Palau-flagged vessel was sailing 20 nautical miles northeast of Omani port of Sohar when the incident occurred.

title: Antonio Gracias: Elon Musk's Friend Set To Be Billionaire After SpaceX IPO
content: Antonio Gracias, one of Musk's closest business allies, is set for a major payday as SpaceX heads to the public markets.

title: Man In Hyderabad Gets Wife Killed Over Sending Money To Her Parents
content: Police said the victim, 36-year-old Mina Devi, was killed on the night of May 30. Her husband had initially told investigators that three men kidnapped his wife and slit her throat when he tried to intervene.

title: Inside Rebel Faction's Strategy To Prove Itself "Real Trinamool"
content: Prakash Chik Baraik resigned as Trinamool Rajya Sabha MP, making him the third Rajya Sabha MP from the party to do so this week.

title: Credit Card Spending Tops Rs 1.5 Lakh Crore, Savings Shrink. Trouble Ahead?
content: Financial planners say a growing number of cardholders pay only the minimum amount due on their monthly credit card bills.

title: Woman Found Dead At Delhi Home, Cops Suspect Husband Of Murder
content: The incident came to light during the early hours when police received information that a man had allegedly assaulted his wife at their home in the Gautampuri area and fled the spot.

title: "Man Must Pay Child Maintenance Even If Unemployed": Bombay High Court
content: The Nagpur Bench of the Bombay High Court was hearing a petition by a man who had challenged a lower court's order to pay Rs 8,000 monthly maintenance to his children.

title: How 'Made In India' Passenger Jets Will Change Indian Aviation Forever
content: India is one of the fastest-growing economies today, and its aviation sector has taken a massive leap.

title: Centre Removes Excise Duty On Higher Ethanol Fuel. What It Means For You
content: The exemption comes as India steadily expands its biofuel programme to reduce dependence on imported crude oil and promote cleaner energy.

title: Nipah alert in Kerala: Kozhikode man's preliminary test comes back positive
content: a 43-year-old man with suspected symptoms of Nipah virus infection was admitted to a hospital in Kozhikode . preliminary tests at the medical college returned positive, indicating a possible infection . health officials are treating it as a suspected case while also initiating precautionary measures .

title: Alive by chance, shattered by fate: AI crash sole survivor battles scars
content: Viswashkumar Ramesh was the luckiest man alive after the crash . his younger brother, Ajay, died in the crash, along with all the other passengers and crew . he escaped because his seat, 11A, was next to an emergency exit .
